title: Topology
subtitle: A free-move map of your server estate — drag to arrange, scroll to zoom.

empty.title: No servers mapped yet
empty.description: Once you add hosts, VMs, containers and their relationships, your estate will appear here as a draggable diagram.

error.title: Couldn't load the topology
error.description: The infra map failed to load. Try again in a moment.

pending.title: Pending review
pending.description: Hosts the reporting agent discovered. Confirm to add them to your inventory, or discard.

confirm.title: Confirm this server
confirm.description: Add this agent-discovered host to your live topology. You can re-classify or rename it first.
confirm.trackAsAssetDescription: On by default — also create a tracked Asset (with the reported host facts) so this server can carry an owner, KB links and secrets. Turn off to keep it graph-only.

onboarding.title: Track servers automatically
onboarding.description: Install a lightweight agent on a Linux server and it reports its hardware & software to lazyit by itself — it can only report, nothing else.
onboarding.noPermissionHint: Ask an administrator to add a reporting agent — it needs the manage-settings permission.

wizard.title: Create a reporting agent
wizard.subtitle: Install a reporting agent on a Linux server in three steps.
wizard.whatIsAgent: An agent is a tiny program you run on a server. It reports the server's hardware and installed software to lazyit by itself — it can't read or change anything else.
wizard.nameHint: A name to recognise this agent later (for example the server's name).
wizard.scopeNote: This token can only report inventory (infra:report). It can't read or change anything else, and every host it reports lands in Pending review for your approval.
wizard.requirements: You'll run this on a Linux server with root access.
wizard.installHint: Copy the command and run it on the Linux server as root. The token is shown only once — capture it before closing.

manual.intro: Prefer to inspect each step? Run these on the server instead of the one-liner above.
manual.step1: Download the binary (use arch=arm64 on ARM machines):
manual.step2: Make it executable and move it into place:
manual.step3: Create the config file (it holds the token, so chmod 600):
manual.step4: Send a first report to check it works:

wizard.waitDescription: Run the install command on your server. As soon as the agent checks in, it'll show up here — usually within a couple of minutes.
wizard.successDescription: Your server checked in. Confirm it to add it to your inventory.

servers.title: Servers
servers.subtitle: Every node in your topology — filter, search and open one to see its owner, secrets and connections.

create.title: Add a node to the map
create.description: Map a host, VM, container or any other part of your estate.
create.trackAsAssetDescription: On by default — the node gets a backing asset so it can carry an owner, KB links and secrets. Turn off for ephemeral nodes like containers.
create.linkAssetSearch: Search by name, tag or serial…
create.linkAssetDescription: Leave empty to create a minimal asset named after the node.

panel.noOwners: No owner — this node isn't tracked as an asset, or no one is assigned.
panel.noSecrets: No secret references. Values are never shown here — only handles.
panel.secretHandleNote: Reference only — the value is never revealed here.
panel.secretPickerEmpty: No secrets found. You only see secrets you can access.
panel.noChildren: Nothing runs on this node yet.
panel.statusDescription: Set the node's live state. The reporting agent will drive this automatically later.
panel.shortcutInvalid: Each shortcut needs a label and a valid URL.
panel.impactDescription: If this node goes down, what stops with it? Highlights the downstream nodes on the map.
panel.impactSafe: Nothing depends on this node — safe to take down.
panel.impactCount: {count, plural, one {# node affected if this goes down} other {# nodes affected if this goes down}}

delete.title: Remove this node from the map?
delete.descriptionSuffix: off the map. Its history is kept and it can be restored later.

edges.title: Connections
edges.description: How this node relates to the rest of your estate.
edges.addDescription: Pick the relationship and the other node.
edges.directionNote: This node is the source; the relationship points to the other node.
